title: carya glabra var. megacarpa
intro: Carya glabra var. megacarpa, also known as the bigleaf hickory, is a deciduous tree species within the Juglandaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The bark and leaves of the bigleaf hickory tree are used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Bigleaf hickory can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile plant for various herbal preparations.
